results.title: N0017827R3105 AI_INTELLIGENT AUTOMATION SUPPORT SERVICES
highlights:
AI_INTELLIGENT AUTOMATION SUPPORT SERVICES
...
# N0017827R3105 AI_INTELLIGENT AUTOMATION SUPPORT SERVICES
...
The Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren Division, under the Department of the Navy, is seeking support for applied artificial intelligence and intelligent automation to integrate advanced automation capabilities into naval systems. The work covers technical and engineering support across the AI/ML lifecycle, including systems engineering, data and configuration management, software and hardware engineering, AI/ML algorithm development, DevSecOps and MLOps, cybersecurity, prototyping, test and evaluation, and related business and program support. The anticipated effort is a cost-plus-fixed-fee, level-of-effort task order with a five-year period of performance, to be performed at NSWCDD and contractor facilities in Dahlgren, Virginia, and the current action is a sources sought/request for information rather than a solicitation. Capability statements of up to 15 pages are requested from current SeaPort-NxG IDIQ MAC holders, including small businesses, by July 9, 2026, and a formal solicitation is anticipated by May 2028.
...
0_Applied Artificial Intelligence-Intelligent Automation_SOW_Final.pdf
...
The Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren Division (NSWCDD) Intelligent Automation Division (H60) requires contractor support for Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) solutions for weapon systems and autonomous platforms. This Statement of Work (SOW) outlines comprehensive technical and engineering services across the AI/ML system lifecycle, from concept to sustainment. Key support areas include program/project management, systems engineering, data and configuration management, computer hardware engineering, prototyping, software engineering, AI/ML algorithm development, test and evaluation (T&E), cybersecurity, safety and ethical assurance, and DevSecOps/MLOps infrastructure engineering. The contractor will implement a modern Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) framework, conduct rigorous Assured AI (AAI) T&E, and ensure compliance with Department of Defense ethical principles for AI. This effort aims to enhance mission effectiveness, increase operational tempo, and reduce risk to human operators by integrating autonomy and AI/ML onto weapon systems.
...
The Naval Surface Warfare Center, Dahlgren Division (NSWCDD) issued an Advance Notice/Sources Sought/Industry Day Notice (N0017827R3105) for H60 Department Applied Artificial Intelligence/Intelligent Automation Support Services. This is not a Request for Proposal, but a request for information for planning purposes. The scope of work involves providing qualified personnel to integrate state-of-the-art automation capabilities into naval systems, encompassing all technical and engineering stages of the acquisition process, including business, financial, management, configuration management, IA/ML Algorithm Development, DevSecOps, machine learning operations, IT Infrastructure Engineering, cybersecurity, and software engineering support. The anticipated contract type is a Cost-Plus-Fixed-Fee, Level of Effort, Task Order, with a five-year period of performance. Work will be performed at NSWCDD and contractor facilities. A set-aside determination has not yet been made. Capability statements, limited to 15 pages, are requested from current SeaPort-NxG IDIQ MAC holders, including small businesses, and must be submitted by July 9, 2026. A formal Solicitation is anticipated by May 2028. An Industry Day is tentatively scheduled for November 2026. The notice also details various labor categories and their respective quantities and locations.

results.title: SAM #N0017827R3105 - NSWC Dahlgren | Govly
highlights:
Comprehensive AI/ML Automation Support Services for Naval Weapon Systems
...
The Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren Division (NSWCDD), under the Department of the Navy, is seeking contractor support for advanced Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) systems development, integration, testing, and sustainment for naval weapon systems. - Government Buyer: - Department of the Navy, Naval Surface Warfare Center Dahlgren Division (NSWCDD), Intelligent Automation Division (H60) - OEMs and Vendors: - No specific OEMs or vendors are named in the solicitation or attachments - Products/Services Requested: - Comprehensive support services for AI/ML-enabled automation, including: - Program and project management - Systems engineering and integration - Hardware and software engineering - AI/ML algorithm development and MLOps pipelines - Cybersecurity and DevSecOps - Safety and ethical assurance for autonomous systems - Configuration and data management - Prototyping, model-making, and low-rate production - Integrated logistics support - Training and instruction - Technical and in-service engineering support - Facilities and secure storage - Labor categories include: Senior Program Manager, Software Engineers, Systems Engineers, Configuration Management Analysts, Electrical Engineers, Test Engineers, Mechanical Engineers, Information System Security Specialists, Systems Administrators, Network Engineers, Logistics Specialists, Technical Specialists, Program Management Analysts, Junior Finance Analyst, Procurement Analyst - Quantities specified for labor categories (e.g., 5 Senior Software Engineers at KR-SITE, 4 Test Engineers at GOV-SITE, etc.) - Unique or Notable Requirements: - Development and integration of autonomous weapon systems - MLOps pipelines and assured AI testing - Ethical AI deployment with human-in-the-loop controls, bias mitigation, and explainability - Secure facilities and classified information handling - Extensive travel to military locations - Support for urgent operational needs and rapid response - Lifecycle sustainment and digital logistics - Training for operators and maintainers, including simulation-based and mobile training - No specific products, part numbers, or OEMs are identified; the focus is on specialized technical and engineering services for AI/ML automation in naval systems.
...
The Contractor shall provide qualified personnel to support the integration of state-of-the-art automation capabilities onto current and future naval systems. The support shall encompass all technical and engineering stages of the acquisition process, from material solution analysis to technical development, development and integration, test and evaluation, and fielding of safe and innovative weapon systems. Other duties indirectly supporting these efforts include providing business operational and financial support, management and analysis support, configuration management support, IA/ML Algorithm Development support, DevSecOps support, machine learning operations support, IT Infrastructure Engineering support, cybersecurity and systems/network, software engineering, system administration, and others. Specific programs or projects to be supported under this Task Order may require tasks to be completed under one or more of these areas.

The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.
...
- As a Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects. You will be expected to follow processes and practices that allow your models to be incorporated into our machine learning platform for production execution and monitoring, however, initial exploratory data analysis allows for more flexible experimentation to discover solutions to the business problems presented.
...
- Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Operations, along with product management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques.
...
- Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases.
...
- Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work.
...
- Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA.
...
- Work with production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance.
...
- Participate in other data science teams collaborating with your peers to support their projects.
...
- Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team.
...
- Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform.
...
- Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption.
...

- PhD or Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI.

- Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases.
...
- 9+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU.
...
- Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP.
...
- Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets.
...
- Passion for collaboration, learn it all mindset and driving value with AI.

Our diverse and innovative team of professionals ranges from skilled trades to project managers, engineers and software developers to solution architects, technical subject matter experts, and system users. # AI Engineer Software (Level 2 or 3)
...
Redondo Beach, CAEl Segundo, CAOn-siteMid-LevelSeniorBackendML / AIClearance Preferred
...
Build production AI/ML solutions including RAG pipelines and LLM-based systems for defense and aerospace applications at Northrop Grumman's Redondo Beach facility.
...
The Northrop Grumman Aeronautics Sector in Space Park Redondo Beach/El Segundo, CA is seeking a highly motivated AI Software Engineer to join our team. Our AI team is growing and there is an opportunity to work closely with senior AI subject matter experts while simultaneously supporting the business. We are looking for a talented AI Software Engineer who can take small to medium sized projects from concept to production quickly with limited technical guidance. You will build and fine‑tune Retrieval‑Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines, Agentic AI pipelines, and large language model (LLM) solutions that solve concrete business problems. You will work closely with product owners, data scientists, and senior engineers to ship reliable, scalable AI services. Successful candidates will have demonstrated the ability to build AI solutions to solve customer problems. They will have a track record as an effective communicator and problem solver who is able to develop and maintain good working relationships with internal and external stakeholders. The selected candidates will work closely with AI engineers and business partners to accomplish the following:
...
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deploy machine learning algorithms for testing and is in production systems.
...
RAG, Agentic AI, and LLM Development – Design and implement retrieval‑augmented generation pipelines and agentic workflows integrating pretrained LLMs (e.g., GPT‑4/5/OSS, Llama) though APIs and self-hosted model deployments.
...
Model Integration – Embed fine‑tuned models into existing micro‑service architectures (REST/GraphQL, gRPC) and expose them via APIs or internal SDKs.
...
Data Preparation – Collaborate with data engineers to curate, clean, and annotate domain‑specific datasets for fine‑tuning and evaluation.
...
Continuous Improvement – Stay current with the latest LLM research, open‑source tools, and industry trends; propose upgrades to the AI stack.
...
For level 2 consideration: Bachelor
...
STEM field with relevant entry level experience

Entry level professional AI/software engineering experience; demonstrated ability to ship production-grade code
...
Demonstrated ability to carry out rapid prototyping and proof-of-concept demonstrations using novel AI methods to solve real-world challenges.
...
Proficiency in Python (including libraries such as PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, LangChain, LangGraph).
...
Familiarity with RAG and agentic AI concepts: vector stores (e.g., Pinecone, FAISS, Milvus), embeddings, similarity search, prompt engineering, function calling or tool use, structured outputs
...
Experience developing AI solutions using LLMs, Agentic AI, MLOps pipelines, Cloud services, ETL, evaluation of AI solution, services, containers and container orchestration
...
Hands‑on experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).
...
### Preferred Qualifications
...
Experience building and training large neural networks on custom datasets
...
Strong understanding of neural network fundamentals, including model architectures, loss functions, optimization, regularization, and evaluation methodologies

Experience with supervised learning, unsupervised learning, reinforcement learning, and statistical modeling

...

...
# Data Scientist
...
Job Title: Data Scientist
...
Location: Monroeville, Pennsylvania
...
As a Data Scientist at BPMI, you will work under normal supervision and perform semi-complex critical technical functions to execute the BPMI mission in areas including, but not limited to applying data analysis, statistical modeling, and machine learning techniques to generate actionable insights that drive business decisions. This role involves collecting, analyzing, and interpreting both structured and unstructured data to support business solutions and operational improvements. Collaboration with cross-functional teams is key to identifying business challenges, developing predictive models, and communicating findings through reports, dashboards, and presentations. Continuous learning and adapting to emerging trends and technologies in data science are essential to fostering a data-driven culture within BPMI. The role ensures data accuracy, optimizes processes for efficiency, and works with stakeholders to enhance decision-making and business performance.
...
Essential Duties:
...
- Prepare, analyze, and interpret data from various sources using statistical analysis, predictive modeling, and computational techniques.
- Develop and maintain predictive models, analytical applications, and data pipelines to support business and engineering solutions.
- Collaborate with data owners to scope problems and implement data-driven solutions.
- Analyze both structured and unstructured data sets, ensuring statistical significance and accuracy in results.
...
- Present findings and insights in clear, accessible visuals such as reports, dashboards, and presentations.
...
- Contribute to knowledge sharing and documentation to promote learning within teams.
- Stay informed on emerging data science trends and technologies to improve methods and techniques.
- Apply data science methods to improve decision-making and operational efficiency.
...
- Participate in software development projects, including writing and testing code.
...
- Implement basic configuration management practices to track changes in code and data.
...
Minimum Job Requirements:
...
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related technical degree from an accredited college or university.
- A minimum of two (2) years of relevant experience.
- Ten (10) or more years of completed education and/or relevant experience may be considered to satisfy educational and years of experience requirements.
- Requires a security clearance; however, all qualified candidates will be considered regardless of their current clearance status. The ability to obtain and maintain a Department of Energy security clearance is required.
...
Preferred Job Requirements
...
- Minimum GPA of 3.0 is preferred for recent college graduates.
...
- Working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint. Outlook, etc.) and other commonly used business applications or role specific applications.
...
- Strong interpersonal
...
Bechtel Plant Machinery, Inc. (BPMI) is a prime contractor for the Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program (NNPP). BPMI is involved in the design, purchase, quality control, and delivery of major propulsion plant components for installation in nuclear-powered aircraft carriers, submarines, and prototype plants for the U.S. Navy. Teamwork, cooperation, and the pursuit of excellence have been the driving forces behind the growth and success of BPMI. For more information, visit www.bpmionline.com.
